text.title: Executive Stakeholder Demo Narrative Frame Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Executive Stakeholder Demo Narrative Frame must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Message has: audience, primary call-to-action, falsifier, and timestamp. Messages without an audience field are rejected.
Drafts go through one review pass before send. Sent messages are archived with their version and the audience that received them.

rationale.source: Press-release / comms QA practice from comms-management literature

rule.rationale: Press-release / comms QA practice from comms-management literature.

rule.statement: Templates are filled, never invented from scratch each time. Template drift (variant-per-sender) is the silent death of comms ops.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Client demo prep + run Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
